claim: A 2021 review found that household, traffic, industry, and agricultural sources vary dramatically by city, making source-specific air-quality interventions more effective than generic measures.

source.title: Source attribution of air pollution in megacities: a systematic review

method: Reviewed source-apportionment methods, city comparisons, and intervention effectiveness.
limitations: Source-apportionment studies use different methods with varying uncertainty.
LMIC megacity studies are sparse relative to high-income cities.
